goals.objective: Reconcile scheduler runtime selection so explicit operator or smoke runtime wins unless blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit mock runtime remains effective runtime for deterministic low-risk smoke.
Adaptive runtime selection applies only when no runtime is requested.
Blocked runtime requests return blocked reasons and start no workers.
swarm-resource-plan.test.ts passes under the updated contract.

goals.objective: Plan a selected integration-origin work item into linked goal, loop and prepared maker/checker leases.
acceptance_criteria: Planning links source event, work item, goal, loop and leases.
Maker and checker leases remain prepared after planning.
Planning does not start a worker.
Operator retry is idempotent or returns a clear duplicate-chain response.

goals.objective: Run maker and checker for an integration-origin work item through the existing worker pool.
acceptance_criteria: Worker start uses existing scheduler gates.
Maker artifacts, gates and trace/checkpoint refs link to source work item.
Checker waits for maker evidence.
Checker verdict links to the loop and source work item.

goals.objective: Close the completed integration-origin loop into eval, reflection and optional memory or repair candidates.
acceptance_criteria: Completed run creates eval and reflection candidate.
Reusable lesson creates memory candidate only.
Regression creates repair work item.
Missing evidence blocks closure.
No automatic durable OKF memory promotion occurs.

goals.objective: Show the integration run chain and next safe action in Mission Control.
acceptance_criteria: Mission Control shows source event, work item, goal, loop, leases, gates, eval and candidates.
Mission Control shows requested runtime vs effective runtime.
Mission Control renders empty and object payloads without map crashes.
Operator can identify next safe action without raw stdout.

goals.objective: Run one deterministic mock-runtime smoke from imported integration event to learning closure.
acceptance_criteria: Full chain completes with evidence ids.
OpenSpec validates strict.
Server focused tests pass.
Dashboard focused tests and build pass.
type-check and git diff check pass.
